mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic expansion, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery in labor markets is a key focus for investors.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it forces the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations.
evidence_refs: doc:FOMC statements from 2004-2006 showing concern over rising inflation.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically lower than in periods where it is not met.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales (RSAFS) are accelerating while new housing construction (HOUST) is decelerating signals a late-cycle economic peak. This indicates weakening in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, leading to negative forward returns as recession risk is priced in.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in RSAFS > 0 while 6-month change in HOUST < 0
evidence_refs: fact:The 2006-2007 period showed this divergence ahead of the GFC.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of RSAFS and HOUST changes does not show a negative coefficient with statistical significance (p > 0.05) for predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, a consistent decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms the recovery is self-sustaining, reducing risk premia and boosting investor confidence, leading to positive returns. This effect diminishes once full employment is reached.
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate peaked in late 2009 and was on a downward trajectory by March 2011.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6.5% and falling, the correlation with 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and business investment, which translates directly to higher corporate revenues and positive earnings surprises, supporting higher equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a core component of many leading economic indicators.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of a 3-month change in INDPRO for predicting 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is held near the zero lower bound, it creates a highly accommodative environment for risk assets by lowering borrowing costs and pushing investors into equities in search of yield.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed adopted a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) in December 2008.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not statistically greater than the long-term average return.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high real GDP growth (GDPC1) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy. Markets anticipate that the central bank will be forced to tighten policy aggressively, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY % change > 3.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 3.0%
failure_condition: In quarters where the trigger condition is met, the subsequent quarter's S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a short period is a robust real-time indicator of a recessionary shock (the 'Sahm Rule'), causing an immediate repricing of risk assets to the downside.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of historical instances.
